title: So i recently did an evangelion marathon
display_title: So i recently did an evangelion marathon
wikitext:
[]: So i recently did an evangelion marathon...             Quote Post by Noobsbane » Tue Jun 18, 2013 11:32 am So i recently did an Evangelion marathon the platinum boxed set ( with direcotrs cut episodes) including the controversial episodes 25 and 26 + death and rebirth + end of evangelion in one sitting, only breaks were to change disc's, now i remember seeing a snip it or 2 of this show when i was a younger lad and through my recent spree of anime collecting i got the evangelion series and was so so so pumped to watch it, and now i know why i was told not to marathon the series ...my mind has gone to mush i cant even comprehend it i think its actually made me kind of depressed like seriously, what is my life, what is reality hnnnngh, What is the actual fuck am i doing with my life... But in short lovely anime the re mastered version with directors cut are quite remarkable and well id suggest the series to anyone but Marathoning it without knowing what your getting into is a huge mistake....ive been walking around like an emotionless zombie for the past day now... In short has anyone else done this and had a similar thing happen to them Shinji "Why can't I dream that I'm alone" Rei "That is not a dream that is a substitute for reality" Shinji "So where is my dream?" Rei "it is a continuation of reality" Shinji "But where is my reality" Rei "It is at the end of your dream"

[]: Re: So i recently did an evangelion marathon...             Quote Post by Stryker » Tue Jun 18, 2013 11:49 am Noobsbane wrote: Shinji "Why can't I dream that I'm alone" Rei "That is not a dream that is a substitute for reality" Shinji "So where is my dream?" Rei "it is a continuation of reality" Shinji "But where is my reality" Rei "It is at the end of your dream"

[]:             Quote Post by Noobsbane » Tue Jun 18, 2013 11:58 am mmm it just really mess's with your head anime's like this with such a heavy physiological aspect to them are magnificent but i dont think anyone can watch this series without feeling a sence of as you so quaintly put it a face fucking :P, on a lighter note looking forward to the 3.33 english dub ? i watched 1.11 and 2.22 afterwards aswell and im undecided as to which one i like more to be honest

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Tue Jun 18, 2013 12:05 pm Although I watched both movies once, I am waiting for all the movies to release before I make a decision like that. Anyways, since this is a chronological story, it's difficult to state which one is the best, due to (if the storytelling is good) all the events relying on each other in order to have happened.

[]: Re: So i recently did an evangelion marathon...             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Jun 18, 2013 5:55 pm Stryker wrote: I still don't know what this means. Anyways, don't worry about it, we were all facefucked at the end of series.

[]:             Quote Post by Noobsbane » Tue Jun 18, 2013 11:13 pm I feel like Marathoning it just was a mistake i think it was abit of an over exposure method for me, you could say that the mixture of NGE + a lack of sleep was like me going to a lower and lower depth in an entry plug haha maybe ive become contaminated who knows :P

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jun 19, 2013 7:28 am I marathoned that sucker once. Never again. That show is too crazy for that.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Wed Jun 19, 2013 9:52 am I did this with a bunch of annons when /a/ streamed the whole thing a year ago. Much DEEP was had.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Wed Jun 19, 2013 1:08 pm Yeah, I love the live action sequence. I think the longer version would have ruined the pacing.

[]: Re: So i recently did an evangelion marathon...             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Jun 19, 2013 7:35 pm Noobsbane wrote: ...my mind has gone to mush i cant even comprehend it i think its actually made me kind of depressed like seriously, what is my life, what is reality hnnnngh, What is the actual fuck am i doing with my life...

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jun 19, 2013 7:57 pm Ever since I fell in love with the series in March 2006 I've marathoned it once a year every March whenever I can schedule a day or two off. I call it my EVAversary. It's a mighty geeky, mind-melding experience. There's always a new discovery I make each time I've rewatched it. Lately I'd added a Day Two which is when I watch the Rebuild films. What there is. Don't know how long I'll keep up the EVAversary though... Maybe until 2016, my ten year marker, then do it every other year or so. Gotta admit, aside from my own work I never thought I'd ever rewatch anything more than 3 or 4 times in my life. Eva proved me wrong though. It's a great thign to marathon.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Wed Jun 19, 2013 9:08 pm Noobsbane wrote: I feel like Marathoning it just was a mistake i think it was abit of an over exposure method for me, you could say that the mixture of NGE + a lack of sleep was like me going to a lower and lower depth in an entry plug haha maybe ive become contaminated who knows :P

[]: Re: So i recently did an evangelion marathon...             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Wed Jun 19, 2013 10:31 pm Shinji "Why can't I dream that I'm alone" Rei "That is not a dream that is a substitute for reality" Shinji "So where is my dream?" Rei "it is a continuation of reality" Shinji "But where is my reality" Rei "It is at the end of your dream" Stryker wrote: I still don't know what this means. Anyways, don't worry about it, we were all facefucked at the end of series.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jun 20, 2013 9:33 am Noobsbane wrote: I feel like Marathoning it just was a mistake i think it was abit of an over exposure method for me, you could say that the mixture of NGE + a lack of sleep was like me going to a lower and lower depth in an entry plug haha maybe ive become contaminated who knows :P

[]: Re: So i recently did an evangelion marathon...             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jun 20, 2013 1:35 pm Noobsbane wrote: ...my mind has gone to mush i cant even comprehend it i think its actually made me kind of depressed like seriously, what is my life, what is reality hnnnngh, What is the actual fuck am i doing with my life...

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Jun 21, 2013 8:33 pm Well, well... another case of the "I've just done an Eva Marathon mode and experiencing something like never before" situation. ...my mind has gone to mush i cant even comprehend it i think its actually made me kind of depressed like seriously, what is my life, what is reality hnnnngh, What is the actual fuck am i doing with my life...

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Jun 21, 2013 8:34 pm You haven't truly experienced Evangelion until you've gotten a 4k monitor and watched the entire series simultaneously. In Klingon

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Jun 21, 2013 9:15 pm tomrule123 wrote: Oh Good God, the thought of doing that right after that would be just...
